data.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Use a venv where `.venv/bin/python` is a symlink to a base interpreter (normal on macOS/Homebrew), AND have another `rebar` earlier on PATH (e.g. `pipx install nava-rebar`).
2. Run any bash suite via pytest: `pytest tests/scripts/test_bash_suites.py`.
3. `_suite_env()` prepends `Path(sys.executable).resolve().parent` — `.resolve()` follows the venv symlink to the BASE interpreter's bin (no venv console scripts), so the bash suites resolve `rebar` to the stale global/pipx install, NOT the editable code under test.

Expected: bash suites exercise the editable `rebar` from `pip install -e` (next to `sys.executable`).
Actual: they run whatever `rebar` is first on the developer's PATH. Detected by test-rebar-push-policy.sh (the old pipx rebar predates the REBAR_PUSH=off policy, so 'off' pushed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] _suite_env uses Path(sys.executable).parent (no .resolve()) so the venv's own bin (with the editable rebar) is first on PATH
- [ ] test-rebar-push-policy.sh passes through pytest
- [ ] Full scripts tier green against the editable rebar
data.title: test_bash_suites runs the wrong rebar binary (_suite_env uses sys.executable.resolve())
